growth_records.strain: Enrichment from high-rate activated sludge, Eawag WWTP Dubendorf (inoculum); reactor COD:P 200
growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 200 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate on COD basis)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.4 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 200 ... active biomass yields ... 0.36 +/- 0.07 gpCOD PHA-free gCOD removed-1 ... PHA yield ... 0.02 +/- 0.01 ... PHA content ... 0.06 +/- 0.01 ... biomass iP 13.8 +/- 1.1 mgP gpCOD-1 ... Full and stable COD-removal (>=95%)
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture (not a single prokaryote) - flagged. Steady-state chemostat, SRT=HRT=1 d => D=1/24 h=0.0417/h; at steady state mu=D=0.0417/h, doubling time=ln2/0.0417=16.6 h. Xanthobacter sp. most abundant PHA-storer (44%+/-34%) at this COD:P; 54% total PHA-storers. Yields community-level per gCOD removed. Volumetric COD uptake = D*ΔCOD ~ (1/d)*(0.95*8.4) ~= 8 gCOD/L/d (not biomass-specific; biomass conc not reported as gCOD/L in text). No secreted products (PHA is intracellular storage).

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 400 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.1 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 400 ... active biomass yield 0.35 +/- 0.04 ... PHA yield 0.07 +/- 0.04 ... PHA content 0.16 +/- 0.09 ... biomass iP 7.9 +/- 1.6
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture - flagged. D=1/24 h=0.0417/h; mu=D; doubling 16.6 h. 62% PHA-storers (17% putative). COD removal >=95%. Yields community-level per gCOD removed.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 600 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.5 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 600 ... active biomass yield 0.31 +/- 0.04 ... PHA yield 0.07 +/- 0.01 ... PHA content 0.18 +/- 0.04 ... biomass iP 5.4 +/- 0.5 ... 81% PHA-storers
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture - flagged. D=mu=0.0417/h; doubling 16.6 h. 81% PHA-storers, stable (CV 4%). COD removal >=95%. Yields community-level.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 800 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (>=95% of 8.0 gCOD/L at D=1/d)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: stable microbial community consisting of >90% PHA-storers dominated by Pannonibacter sp. ... active biomass yields lowest ... 0.30 +/- 0.03 ... PHA yield highest 0.18 +/- 0.03 ... PHA content 0.36 +/- 0.05 ... biomass iP 4.0 +/- 0.3
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture (>90% PHA-storers, Pannonibacter phragmitetus dominant 78%) - flagged. Reported SRT=HRT ~1.1 d; using nominal 1 d, D=mu=0.0417/h, doubling 16.6 h. Optimal selection condition. Lowest active-biomass yield (P-limited growth) with highest PHA storage. Yields community-level per gCOD removed. COD removal >=95%.

growth_records.medium: Synthetic wastewater, COD:P 1000 gCOD/gP (50% acetate / 50% propionate)

up.units: gCOD/L/d removed (60-90% of 8.0 gCOD/L at D=1/d; unstable)

growth_records.cite: Brison, Antoine et al. Influent carbon to phosphorus ratio drives the selection of PHA-storing organisms in a single CSTR. Water Research X (2022). DOI:10.1016/j.wroa.2022.100150. [PMC9364015]
growth_records.snippet: COD:P 1000 ... COD-removal ... unstable ... fluctuated between 60 and 90% ... active biomass yield 0.34 +/- 0.07 ... PHA yield 0.13 +/- 0.03 ... PHA content 0.27 +/- 0.09 ... biomass iP 3.9 +/- 0.9
growth_records.notes: MIXED microbial culture - flagged. D=mu=0.0417/h; doubling 16.6 h. P-only-limited: selection deteriorated, 57% PHA-storers, Achromobacter 18%. COD removal unstable 60-90% (uptake rate ~0.75*8=6 gCOD/L/d approximate). Yields community-level.

growth_records.strain: Laboratory-scale SBR enrichment (inoculum: activated sludge from Roma Nord full-scale municipal WWTP, Rome, Italy)
growth_records.medium: Mineral medium with acetic/propionic acid feast substrate (respiration experiments: acetic acid / sodium acetate pulses)

up.compound: acetate (acetic acid, as COD) - maximum specific substrate uptake rate qsm (saturating Csi)
up.method: O2 + COD dissolved-oxygen mass-balance modelling of pulse respiration experiments (n=24); qsm = qsr + qsu (7.4 + 21.1)

up.compound: acetate (acetic acid, as COD) - resting/baseline specific substrate uptake rate qsr
acetate (acetic acid, as COD) - maximum upshift increment in specific substrate uptake rate qsu

up.method: reference endogenous respiration from O2 mass balance (n=24), 0.64±0.12

up.compound: O2 (substrate-associated respiration qos = Yos*qs at qsm)
up.method: calculated qos = Yos*qsm = 0.26 gO2/gCOD * 28.5 mgCOD/gVSS/min = 7.41 mgO2/gVSS/min

yields.note: substrate is stored as PHB, not secreted; no biomass growth assumed during feast storage response

growth_records.cite: Werker A et al. Modelling Mixed Microbial Culture Polyhydroxyalkanoate Accumulation Bioprocess towards Novel Methods for Polymer Production Using Dilute Volatile Fatty Acid Rich Feedstocks. Bioengineering (Basel) (2022). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering9030125. [PMC8945694]
growth_records.snippet: Xa 1.0±0.1 gVSS/L; qoe 0.64±0.12 mgO2/gVSS/min; Yos 0.26±0.02 gO2/gCOD; ks 2.0±0.7 mgCOD/L; ku 38±7 mgCOD/L; two mgCOD/gVSS/min rows 7.4±0.1 and 21.1±1.1 (Table 1, Laboratory Scale, active aeration, n=24). Temperature 24.6±0.3 °C, pH(t=0) 9
growth_records.notes: Laboratory-scale MMC. All values from Table 1 (active-aeration respiration experiments, mean±SD, n in brackets). Measured max specific ACETATE uptake capacity: qsm = qsr + qsu (Eq 9, at saturating Csi) = 7.4 + 21.1 = 28.5 mgCOD/gVSS/min. Unit conversion to mmol acetate/gVSS/h: 28.5 mgCOD/gVSS/min * 60 = 1710 mgCOD/gVSS/h; acetic-acid COD factor 1.066 gCOD/g => 1710/1.066 = 1604 mg acetic acid/gVSS/h = 1604/60 = 26.7 mmol acetate/gVSS/h (max storage-driven uptake). qos computed as Yos*qsm = 0.26*28.5 = 7.41 mgO2/gVSS/min. Yos 0.26 gO2/gCOD matches theoretical 0.26 for PHA storage without growth. ks=2.0 mgCOD/L (active aeration; 0.110 mgCOD/L at low substrate, no aeration). Growth: enrichment SBR HRT=SRT=1 d => D=1/24=0.0417 h-1; doubling td=ln2/0.0417=16.6 h (community turnover, NOT measured growth during the non-growing storage respiration experiments). Substrate = acetic acid/sodium acetate pulses. Product PHB is stored intracellularly (0.63-0.70 gPHA/gVSS potential), not secreted, so secretion_rates empty.

growth_records.strain: Pilot-scale SBR enrichment (inoculum: activated sludge from Treviso full-scale WWTP, NE Italy)
growth_records.medium: Fermentate filtrate from 33:67 v/v OFMSW + waste activated sludge (75% soluble COD as VFA, dominated by butyric and acetic acids); respiration experiments run with acetic acid/Na-acetate pulses in same matrix

up.compound: acetate (acetic acid, as COD) - maximum specific substrate uptake rate qsm (saturating Csi)
up.method: O2 + COD mass-balance modelling of pulse respiration experiments (n=17); qsm = qsr + qsu (2.5 + 6.8)

up.compound: acetate (acetic acid, as COD) - resting/baseline specific substrate uptake rate qsr
acetate (acetic acid, as COD) - maximum upshift increment qsu

up.method: reference endogenous respiration from O2 mass balance (n=17), 0.25±0.07

growth_records.cite: Werker A et al. Modelling Mixed Microbial Culture Polyhydroxyalkanoate Accumulation Bioprocess towards Novel Methods for Polymer Production Using Dilute Volatile Fatty Acid Rich Feedstocks. Bioengineering (Basel) (2022). DOI:10.3390/bioengineering9030125. [PMC8945694]
growth_records.snippet: Pilot Scale (Table 1, active aeration): Temperature 23.8±0.6 °C; pH(t=0) 9.0±0.1; Xa 1.71±0.04 gVSS/L; ka 0.60±0.15 1/min; qoe 0.25±0.07 mgO2/gVSS/min; Yos 0.23±0.05 gO2/gCOD; ks 1.8±0.4 mgCOD/L; ku 20±2 mgCOD/L; rate rows 2.5±0.2 and 6.8±0
growth_records.notes: Pilot-scale MMC. Values from Table 1 (active-aeration respiration experiments, mean±SD, n=17). qsm = qsr + qsu = 2.5 + 6.8 = 9.3 mgCOD/gVSS/min. Conversion: 9.3*60=558 mgCOD/gVSS/h; /1.066=523 mg acetic acid/gVSS/h = 8.7 mmol acetate/gVSS/h. Xa=1.71 gVSS/L. Medium is a complex fermentate matrix (VFA butyrate+acetate dominated), so composition not fully stated; respiration pulses used acetic acid/Na-acetate. Growth rate = SBR SRT-derived D=1/24=0.0417 h-1 (community turnover, not measured growth during storage experiments). PHB stored intracellularly (0.40-0.46 gPHA/gVSS), not secreted.
